speaker: alice
text: Right, let's talk plastic because this is genuinely game-changing news. Scientists have engineered an enzyme cocktail that completely breaks down polyethylene in just 48 to 72 hours under normal conditions. We're talking about the most produced and most persistent plastic type on Earth, the stuff that makes up 30% of all plastic production.

speaker: charlie
text: This is massive, Jamie. For context, until now there was literally no natural way to break this stuff down. It just sat there for centuries being stubborn. But these clever clogs built on work from a bacterium called Ideonella sakaiensis and basically taught it some new tricks.

speaker: alice
text: And here's the beautiful part - this enzymatic recycling can handle all the contaminated, mixed-up plastic streams that current recycling systems just reject. Meanwhile, ocean alkalinity enhancement trials are moving from lab to open water, amplifying the ocean's natural ability to absorb CO2 without the land-use conflicts of tree planting. Nature's getting some serious backup.
